title: No method found for type
explanation: This error occurs when you call a method that does not exist on a type. This could mean the method does not exist, the trait providing the method is not in scope, or you are calling it on the wrong type.
error_message: error[E0599]: no method named `push` found for reference `&str` in the current scope

fixes.code:
let mut s = String::from("hello");
s.push('!'); // String has push, &str does not

fixes.description: Import the trait that provides the method
Make the variable mutable for mutating methods
fixes.code:
let mut v = vec![3, 1, 2];
v.sort(); // sort() requires &mut self

tip: If the method exists but is not found, check if the trait needs to be imported with `use`. Many iterator methods need `use std::iter::Iterator` (though it's in the prelude).
